infobox.title: Privacy Stack
infobox.imageCaption: GL.iNet router with AdGuard Home running network-wide DNS filtering

lead:
The **Privacy Stack** is a layered personal privacy and security setup spanning OS hardening, browser fingerprint resistance, hardware-level network filtering, self-hosted services, and planned email migration. It currently sits at approximately **85% completeness** — the main remaining gap is Gmail, which has not yet been migrated to a privacy-respecting provider.

The stack is built entirely on free and open source software, which connects philosophically to [[foss-coin|FOSS Coin]] and the broader FOSS-first approach documented across the [[about|profile]]. Every layer is either already deployed or has a concrete plan for deployment through the [[homelab|Libre Cloud homelab]].

content.content:
The operating system layer is the foundation. Running **Arch Linux via [[linux-setup|Omarchy]]** provides the most control over what software is installed and what runs in the background. Hyprland Wayland tiling WM has minimal background services by design — compared to a typical desktop environment, the attack surface and telemetry footprint are dramatically reduced.

Key decisions at this layer:
- **Chromium removed** (telemetry) — replaced with LibreWolf
- **Spotify removed** (tracking) — no replacement
- **Mac address randomization** enabled on all network interfaces
- **Fork control**: upstream Omarchy changes are selectively applied via a personal GitHub fork

content.title: Trust model
content.content: Arch packages flow through the Arch pacman ecosystem, not DHH's servers. Even when using an opinionated Arch distribution like Omarchy, the underlying package signatures and build infrastructure remain the Arch Linux project's — not a third party's.

**LibreWolf** is a Firefox fork with hardened anti-fingerprinting defaults applied out of the box. Key differences from stock Firefox:
- `resistFingerprinting` enabled by default — canvas, WebGL, audio, timezone, and font fingerprinting are all blocked
- No telemetry or crash reporting sent to Mozilla
- Stricter cookie defaults and cross-site isolation

**uBlock Origin** runs on top of LibreWolf in medium mode — blocking all third-party scripts and frames by default, with manual allowlist rules added over time for sites that require scripts to function.

Firefox Multi-Account Containers was also explored for session isolation per site category (banking in one container, social media in another, shopping in another).

content.title: Dark theme fix
content.content: LibreWolf's resistFingerprinting setting can break dark mode rendering in some sites. Fix: toggle resistFingerprinting off and on in about:config to restore correct theming. Known quirk.
The DNS layer is the most technically layered part of the stack, running multiple filtering systems at different levels:

rows.Scope: AdGuard Home on home subnet — blocks ads/malware for all devices
Ads + Trackers + Malware filtering on laptop/mobile
Planned — encrypts traffic from device to homelab exit node

content.title: College WiFi constraint
content.content: SonicWALL SSL inspection on the college WiFi blocks pacman package downloads. Workaround: mobile hotspot for system updates on campus.

rows.Status: ❌ Pending — main gap. Custom domain contact@lingadevaru.in active.
✅ Live — no fingerprinting, no Google Analytics on any owned property
